hypothesis: Si–X alloy anodes whose Li+ migration-channel radius changes by ≤0.45 Å per 100% state-of-charge (|dRt/dSOC|, computed on relaxed structures) will, when fabricated as 70:20:10 active:carbon:binder composite electrodes from 1–3 µm particles, exhibit ≥85% capacity retention after 500 cycles at 1C and ≤15% electrode thickness swelling in 1 M LiPF6 EC/EMC (3:7) with 10% FEC.
